svg.title: Transmit signal chain - all band paths
svg.desc: The full VHF/UHF transmit chain. A 7020-SDR exciter feeds the PE42512A SP12T band-select at low level, which fans out to all twelve ports. Each populated port is a band path: bandpass filter, CGH40010 driver, a power final chosen by frequency (MRF101AN LDMOS for 4 m/2 m/1.25 m, CGH40120F GaN for 70 cm/33 cm/23 cm, microwave final TBD for 13 cm/9 cm/5 cm), then a low-pass filter to a 50 W RF output. Two slices have no amateur allocation (spare) and one port is a low-level dummy/park. Every path ends at its LPF - the node boundary; antenna routing is station-defined. All IARU regions; build the bands your country allocates.
svg.text: Transmit signal chain – all band paths (4 m – 5.8 GHz)
7020-SDR exciter → PE42512A SP12T band-select (low level) → per-band module → LPF. Each path ends at its LPF (node boundary); build only the bands your region allocates.
spare – this 1.5:1 slice has no amateur allocation; leave unpopulated
spare – this 1.5:1 slice has no amateur allocation; leave unpopulated
low-level 50 Ω dummy / TX-inhibit park – cold-switch target
Every path’s driver + final sit inside the PA control / protection wrapper (T/R sequencing, gate bias, SWR + thermal foldback) – see control-wrapper.svg.
Node boundary: each path ends at its LPF. Routing past it (antenna, triplexer, switch, dummy load + RF-exposure lockout) is station-defined.
microwave final TBD (13 cm / 9 cm / 5 cm)
Band-box colour = IARU region availability (R1 blue / all-region teal / R2 purple / varies grey), matching band-coverage.svg. Ports loss-tiered (RF1/RF12 lowest loss). See docs/regions.md.
